card.indications: Treatment of prostate cancer, endometriosis, uterine fibroids, and central precocious puberty depending on formulation.
card.mechanism: Initially stimulates and then suppresses pituitary gonadotropin release, reducing gonadal sex-hormone production.
card.dosing: Dose and interval are formulation- and indication-specific. Depot products are not interchangeable.
card.routeAdministration: Verify exact product and dosing interval. Rotate injection sites and do not substitute depot strengths.
card.contraindications: Pregnancy, undiagnosed vaginal bleeding for some indications, and hypersensitivity to GnRH analogs.
card.warnings: Initial hormone flare can temporarily worsen symptoms. Other risks include bone loss, hyperglycemia, cardiovascular events, QT prolongation, seizures, and fetal harm.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Hot flashes, sweating, headache, mood changes, injection-site reaction, and decreased libido.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Spinal cord compression, urinary obstruction, severe hyperglycemia, cardiovascular events, seizures, and anaphylaxis.
card.interactions: QT-prolonging drugs may increase arrhythmia risk. Diabetes therapy may need adjustment.
card.monitoring: Monitor symptoms, hormone levels or disease markers, bone density with prolonged use, glucose, mood, and cardiovascular status.
card.patientTeaching: Symptoms may briefly worsen at treatment start. Report new weakness, urinary retention, severe bone pain, chest pain, or mood changes.
card.nutrition: Ensure adequate calcium and vitamin D and follow weight-bearing exercise guidance when appropriate.

card.indications: Treatment of acromegaly, severe secretory diarrhea from carcinoid or VIPoma, and selected management of variceal bleeding or other specialist indications.
card.mechanism: Suppresses release of growth hormone and multiple gastrointestinal hormones and reduces splanchnic blood flow.
card.dosing: Dose depends on indication and formulation. Short-acting and depot products use different schedules.
card.routeAdministration: Rotate subcutaneous sites. Allow refrigerated solution to reach room temperature before injection when appropriate. Depot injections require deep IM administration.
card.warnings: Can cause gallstones, glucose disturbances, bradycardia, conduction abnormalities, hypothyroidism, and malabsorption.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhea, constipation, gas, injection-site pain, and headache.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Pancreatitis, severe bradycardia, arrhythmia, biliary obstruction, severe hyperglycemia or hypoglycemia, and anaphylaxis.
card.interactions: May alter insulin or oral-diabetes medication needs. Beta blockers and other rate-slowing drugs increase bradycardia risk.
card.monitoring: Monitor glucose, heart rate, thyroid function, gallbladder symptoms, liver tests, growth hormone or disease markers, and stool response.
card.patientTeaching: Report severe abdominal pain, yellowing skin, fainting, or symptoms of high or low glucose.
card.nutrition: Small frequent meals may reduce GI effects. Monitor fat-soluble vitamin status with prolonged malabsorption.

card.indications: Reduction of infection risk from febrile neutropenia in patients receiving myelosuppressive chemotherapy and selected radiation-injury indications.
card.mechanism: Stimulates proliferation and differentiation of neutrophil precursors and increases neutrophil production and function.
card.dosing: Common adult chemotherapy prophylaxis is a single 6-mg subcutaneous dose per cycle, timed according to the chemotherapy regimen. Pediatric dosing may be weight based.
card.routeAdministration: Do not administer too close to cytotoxic chemotherapy. Follow exact timing and on-body injector instructions.
card.contraindications: Serious allergy to pegfilgrastim or filgrastim products.
card.warnings: Can cause splenic rupture, acute respiratory distress syndrome, severe allergic reactions, sickle-cell crisis, glomerulonephritis, capillary leak syndrome, and leukocytosis.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Bone pain, limb pain, headache, and injection-site reaction.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Splenic rupture, ARDS, anaphylaxis, severe sickle-cell crisis, capillary leak syndrome, and aortitis.
card.interactions: No major metabolic interactions; timing with chemotherapy is critical.
card.monitoring: Monitor CBC with differential, fever or infection, left upper abdominal or shoulder pain, breathing, and allergic reactions.
card.patientTeaching: Report left upper abdominal or shoulder pain, shortness of breath, swelling, dizziness, or severe rash immediately.
card.nutrition: Maintain adequate hydration, calories, and protein during chemotherapy recovery.

card.indications: Treatment of selected B-cell malignancies and autoimmune diseases, including certain lymphomas, leukemia, rheumatoid arthritis, and vasculitis.
card.mechanism: Binds CD20 on B lymphocytes and causes B-cell depletion through immune-mediated mechanisms.
card.dosing: Regimen is disease-, product-, and combination-specific. Premedication is commonly required.
card.routeAdministration: Screen for hepatitis B before treatment. Premedicate as ordered and monitor closely during infusion, especially the first dose.
card.warnings: Boxed warnings include fatal infusion reactions, severe mucocutaneous reactions, hepatitis B reactivation, and progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Infusion reaction, fever, chills, fatigue, nausea, infection, and low blood counts.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Anaphylaxis, tumor lysis syndrome, severe infection, hepatitis B reactivation, PML, severe skin reactions, and cardiac arrhythmia.
card.interactions: Other immunosuppressants increase infection risk. Live vaccines should generally be avoided.
card.monitoring: Monitor vital signs during infusion, CBC, hepatitis B markers, infection, renal function in tumor lysis risk, neurologic changes, and cardiac symptoms.
card.patientTeaching: Report fever, rash, mouth sores, yellowing skin, confusion, weakness, or vision changes immediately.
card.nutrition: Maintain hydration and adequate protein and calories; follow infection-prevention food guidance when neutropenic.

card.indications: Treatment of HER2-positive breast cancer and selected HER2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal cancers.
card.mechanism: Binds the HER2 receptor, inhibiting signaling and promoting immune-mediated destruction of HER2-overexpressing tumor cells.
card.dosing: Loading and maintenance doses are weight- and regimen-specific. IV and subcutaneous products are not automatically interchangeable.
card.routeAdministration: Confirm HER2-positive status and baseline cardiac function. Monitor during infusion for reactions.
card.warnings: Boxed warnings include cardiomyopathy, infusion reactions, pulmonary toxicity, and embryo-fetal toxicity.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Fever, chills, nausea, diarrhea, fatigue, headache, and infusion reaction.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Heart failure, reduced ejection fraction, anaphylaxis, interstitial lung disease, severe pulmonary edema, and fetal harm.
card.interactions: Anthracyclines and other cardiotoxic drugs increase cardiac risk.
card.monitoring: Monitor left ventricular ejection fraction at baseline and intervals, infusion reactions, pulmonary symptoms, pregnancy status, and treatment response.
card.patientTeaching: Report shortness of breath, swelling, rapid weight gain, cough, chest pain, or severe infusion symptoms promptly.
card.nutrition: Maintain adequate calories and protein and manage nausea or diarrhea with individualized oncology nutrition guidance.
